segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a bright yellow screen displaying 'Viu Viu' in red, accompanied by a Captain America shield icon, set to upbeat electronic music. This transitions into a live broadcast of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019, as indicated by on-screen graphics and the FOX logo. A male commentator announces the 'teams making their way out onto the field as the US finally gets set to open up play here at the 2019 FIFA Women's World Cup,' while a wide aerial view shows the stadium. The USA team in red and the Thai team in light blue emerge from a tunnel, flanked by young mascots and officials carrying large national flags, to the sound of crowd cheering and an anthemic musical build-up. Close-ups reveal USA players like Megan Rapinoe, with her distinctive pink hair and jersey #15, and Alex Morgan in jersey #13. Rapinoe, as captain, shakes hands with the referee and the Thai captain, symbolizing sportsmanship, while the commentator notes Rapinoe is 'playing in her third World Cup.' The USA team warms up, with Rapinoe stretching and jogging. The camera briefly cuts to the focused USA coaching staff on the bench, including a coach in a gray USA-crested jacket, identified by the commentator as 'Jill Ellis, head coach.' The commentator also mentions 'Thailand' is playing in their 'second straight World Cup,' as the electric stadium atmosphere, filled with anticipation and crowd sounds, continues.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a bright yellow screen displaying the text 'Viu Viu' in red, accompanied by a Captain America shield icon, suggesting a playful or thematic introduction. The scene then transitions to a live broadcast of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019, as indicated by the on-screen graphics and the FOX logo. The camera captures a wide aerial view of a stadium where two teams, one in red (USA) and the other in light blue (Thailand), are lining up on the field for the pre-match ceremony. The teams walk out through a tunnel, flanked by young mascots and officials, with large national flags of the USA and Thailand being carried on the field. The crowd is visible in the stands, waving flags and cheering. The players, including USA’s Megan Rapinoe with pink hair and Alex Morgan wearing jersey #13, are shown in close-up as they prepare for the match. The captains, Rapinoe and the Thai captain, shake hands with the referee and each other, symbolizing sportsmanship. The USA team is seen warming up, with Rapinoe stretching and jogging. The camera briefly cuts to the USA coaching staff on the bench, including a coach in a gray jacket with a USA crest, who appears focused and engaged. The atmosphere is electric, with the stadium filled with anticipation for the upcoming match.

audio_updated_entities.Megan Rapinoe: USA player with distinctive pink hair, wearing a red jersey with the name 'RAPINOE' and number 15, seen shaking hands with the referee and warming up on the field. A commentator notes she is playing in her third World Cup.
audio_updated_entities.USA Coaching Staff: Coach in a gray USA-branded jacket seated on the bench, displaying focused expressions and hand gestures during the pre-game moments. The commentator identifies the head coach as Jill Ellis.
audio_updated_entities.Thai National Team: Players in light blue jerseys, seen lining up and participating in the pre-match handshake with the USA team. A commentator mentions this is their second straight World Cup appearance.
audio_updated_entities.FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, indicating the tournament and location, explicitly confirmed by a commentator as the '2019 FIFA Women's World Cup'.
audio_updated_entities.Stadium Crowd: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere, with their cheers and stadium sounds clearly audible throughout the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with close-ups of focused USA players in red jerseys, including Alex Morgan (number 13) and a blonde player (number 3), as an announcer references past "World Cups." The announcer then introduces a Thai player, "Noon Gatai Satangian," returning for her second time. The scene transitions to a wide aerial view of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between the USA in red and Thailand in light blue, with an excited announcer exclaiming, "Here we go!" The match begins with the USA attacking, and a shot on goal is taken, described by an announcer as "shooting it up," which the Thai goalkeeper saves after "bobbling" it. The action continues with the USA pressing forward, and a shot from "Mewis" (USA Player #3) is mentioned. The crowd's cheers intensify as a goal is scored, with an announcer excitedly detailing the play: "goalkeeper's coming out, look confused, headed, knocked in! US leads!" Alex Morgan celebrates with a joyful leap and high-fives teammates, and the announcer confirms, "Looks like Alex Morgan will get credit for that." The referee signals the goal, and the scoreboard updates to reflect the score. The electric atmosphere is palpable with the stadium crowd visible, waving flags and reacting to the play, alongside branded banners from sponsors like VISA and Qatar Airways.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with close-ups of USA players in red jerseys, including Alex Morgan (number 13) and another player with blonde hair (number 3), both displaying focused expressions as they prepare for the match. The scene transitions to a wide aerial view of the pitch during the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019, showing the USA in red and Thailand in light blue jerseys. The match begins with the USA attacking, and a shot on goal is taken, which the Thai goalkeeper saves. The action continues with the USA pressing forward, and a goal is scored by Alex Morgan, who celebrates with a joyful leap and high-fives teammates. The referee signals the goal, and the scoreboard updates to reflect the score. The crowd in the stadium is visible, waving flags and reacting to the play. The atmosphere is electric, with the stadium filled with spectators and branded banners from sponsors like VISA and Qatar Airways. The sequence captures the intensity and excitement of the early moments of the match, highlighting key players and pivotal moments like the goal and celebration.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the USA advancing the ball into Thailand's half, as Commentator 1 describes a "big cross" intended "for Morgan," which then falls "to Rapinoe," with "Lavelle" leading the attack before it's "cut out." The USA maintains pressure, and Commentator 1 excitedly narrates "O'Hara chipping it, back post header, GOAL!" The camera captures Alex Morgan scoring with a powerful strike, then leaping in joyful celebration with her arms raised, as Commentator 1 confirms, "Alex Morgan, this one will count." The scoreboard updates to USA 1–0 THA at 13:33, triggering an eruption of intense crowd cheering and a stadium roar. A woman in a red USA jersey is seen screaming in excitement amidst the jubilant Stadium Crowd, who wave flags. Replays show the ball entering the net past the diving but unsuccessful Thai Goalkeeper, and a slow-motion view of Morgan's strike. USA players, including USA Player #3, rush to embrace and high-five Morgan in celebration, while the Thai National Team players react with disappointment and the Referee Team signals the goal. Commentator 2 provides analysis, praising "such a good ball by Kelley O'Hara" and her "aggressive touch" that allowed her to "blow by the first defender," also noting Megan Rapinoe's earlier attempt to "drive in line." The scene shifts to a later moment where Kelley O'Hara, a USA player in red, slides to intercept the ball from a Thai player in light blue, a "challenge" highlighted by Commentator 2. The match continues with the USA maintaining pressure, visible in a wide shot of the field, as on-screen graphics display the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 tournament name, score, time, and advertisements for VISA, Qatar Airways, and KIA along the sidelines. Commentator 1 briefly notes a turnover, "giving it away in a bad spot."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a pivotal moment in the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between the USA and Thailand. The action begins with the USA in possession, advancing the ball into Thailand's half. A well-executed pass leads to a scoring opportunity, culminating in a goal by Alex Morgan, who is seen leaping in celebration with her arms raised. The scoreboard updates to USA 1–0 THA at 13:33, triggering an eruption of joy from the Stadium Crowd, including a woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement. The camera cuts to a replay showing the goal from multiple angles: a close-up of the ball entering the net past the Thai Goalkeeper, who dives but fails to stop it, and a slow-motion view of Morgan’s powerful strike. The USA players, including USA Player #3, rush to celebrate with Morgan, embracing and high-fiving. The Thai National Team players react with disappointment, while the Referee Team signals the goal. The scene shifts to a later moment where a USA player in red slides to intercept the ball from a Thai player in light blue, showcasing intense defensive effort. The match continues with the USA maintaining pressure, as seen in a wide shot of the field with players positioned for an attack. Throughout, the stadium is filled with fans waving flags, and on-screen graphics display the tournament name, score, and time, with advertisements for VISA, Qatar Airways, and KIA visible along the sidelines.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of the USA Coaching Staff, a woman in a black jacket, seated in the stands and intently observing the match. On the field, the USA team in red jerseys is in possession, advancing against the Thai National Team in light blue, with the scoreboard showing USA 1-0 THA at 17:39. Commentary notes a previous shot was wide and mentions Sam Mewis. The action intensifies as Alex Morgan, wearing red jersey #13, visually scores a powerful goal with a strike, sending the ball past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. Simultaneously, the commentator exclaims, "Shot taken! And Rose Lavelle made the right decision. Two nothing US!", attributing the goal to Rose Lavelle. The score updates to USA 2-0 THA at 20:27. The stadium erupts with sustained crowd cheering and stadium sounds, including a woman in a red USA jersey screaming joyfully. Alex Morgan leaps in celebration, high-fiving teammates, including USA Player #3, who embraces her warmly. The Referee Team, in bright green, signals the goal, and the VAR Assistant Referee raises her flag, indicating a possible penalty check. Multiple replays of the goal from different angles confirm the ball crossing the line. The commentator further praises, "And it's a golden left foot, a Rose Lavelle," also mentioning Kelly O'Hara for her speed and Tobin Heath in subsequent plays. The Thai National Team players react with disappointment, while the USA players continue their jubilant celebration. The atmosphere is electric, with banners for 'Qatar Airways' and 'Coca-Cola' visible along the sidelines, and on-screen graphics consistently display 'FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019' as the time ticks from 17:39 to 23:28. Later, the commentator mentions Megan Rapinoe and Sam Mewis again, noting a non-call by the referee after Mewis went down.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of the USA Coaching Staff, a woman in a black jacket holding a clipboard, seated in the stands, observing the match intently. The scene quickly shifts to the field, where the USA team, in red jerseys, is in possession, advancing against the Thai National Team in light blue. The scoreboard shows USA 1-0 THA at 17:39. The action intensifies as Alex Morgan, wearing red jersey #13, scores a goal with a powerful strike, sending the ball past the Thai Goalkeeper in yellow, who dives but fails to stop it. The score updates to USA 2-0 THA at 20:27. The crowd erupts in celebration, with a woman in a red USA jersey screaming joyfully. Alex Morgan leaps in celebration, high-fiving teammates, including USA Player #3, who embraces her warmly. The Referee Team, in bright green, signals the goal, and the VAR Assistant Referee raises her flag, indicating a possible penalty check. The camera captures multiple replays of the goal from different angles, including a close-up of the ball crossing the line and the goalkeeper’s dive. The Thai National Team players react with disappointment, while the USA players continue their jubilant celebration. The stadium is filled with energy, with banners for 'Qatar Airways' and 'Coca-Cola' visible along the sidelines. The on-screen graphics consistently display 'FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019' and the live score, with the time ticking from 17:39 to 23:28. The atmosphere is electric, with the crowd’s excitement palpable as the USA extends their lead.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic sequence from the FIFA Women's World Cup, as a Thai player in a light blue jersey attempts to intercept the ball near the sideline, with a USA player (number 3) in red close by. Commentary discusses a potential VAR 'check or not,' while the 'USA fans booing' are distinctly audible. The scene quickly shifts to a split-screen VAR review, showing the FIFA VAR Room and on-field footage of a USA player (number 9) falling near the goal after a challenge, prompting the referee in bright green to signal for a review. Commentary then describes a separate play where the ball is 'cut wide' and 'curls it, but it's right at Alisson Air.' The action resumes with the USA team advancing, and a foul is called 'outside the penalty area,' leading to a free-kick. From approximately 'a half yards away,' the ball is sent in 'low,' becoming 'loose' before the commentator exclaims, 'And there's one for the US! It's three nothing Horan!' The scoreboard updates to 3-0 as the stadium erupts in loud cheers and excitement. USA players, including Megan Rapinoe (number 15) with distinctive pink hair and Alex Morgan (number 13), are seen erupting in joyful celebration, hugging and jumping. Close-ups capture the jubilant players, with USA Player #3 (blonde hair) embracing Alex Morgan. The stadium crowd, waving American and Thai flags, reacts with palpable excitement, and a woman in a red USA jersey is seen screaming in celebration amidst the roaring crowd. On-screen graphics display the match time, score, and tournament branding, including Coca-Cola, Qatar Airways, and Orange advertisements along the pitch. The sequence concludes with replays of the goal from multiple angles, highlighting the moment the ball crosses the line despite the Thai goalkeeper in yellow diving unsuccessfully.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a dynamic sequence from the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between the USA and Thailand, with the scoreline progressing from 2-0 to 3-0 in favor of the USA. The action begins with a Thai player in a light blue jersey attempting to intercept the ball near the sideline, while a USA player in red (number 3) is seen in close proximity. The scene quickly shifts to a VAR review, shown via a split-screen with the FIFA VAR Room and on-field footage, as a USA player in red (number 9) is seen falling near the goal after a challenge. The referee, in a bright green uniform, signals for a review. The play resumes with the USA team advancing, and a free-kick is taken from the right side of the penalty area. The ball is struck powerfully toward the goal, and the Thai goalkeeper in yellow dives but fails to save it, resulting in a goal. The scoreboard updates to 3-0. The USA players, including the player with pink hair (Megan Rapinoe, number 15) and Alex Morgan (number 13), erupt in celebration, hugging and jumping in joy. The camera captures close-ups of the jubilant players, including USA Player #3 (blonde hair, number 3) embracing Alex Morgan. The stadium crowd, waving American and Thai flags, reacts with excitement, and a woman in a red USA jersey is seen screaming in celebration. The on-screen graphics display the match time, score, and tournament branding, including Coca-Cola, Qatar Airways, and Orange advertisements along the pitch. The sequence ends with a replay of the goal from multiple angles, highlighting the moment the ball crosses the line and the goalkeeper’s unsuccessful dive.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment captures the final moments of the first half of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match between the USA and Thailand, with the scoreline at 3-0 in favor of the USA, set against the constant buzz and cheers of the stadium crowd. The action begins with the USA in possession, advancing the ball into Thailand's half, as a commentator describes "Done" keeping it low and "Morgan turning," attempting a shot that is blocked by the Thai defense. The camera follows the play closely, showing a Thai defender in light blue intercepting the ball and clearing it, while the commentator notes "nine shots for the US, five on goal." The USA continues to press, with multiple players in red jerseys making runs toward the goal, and the commentator highlights "Lavelle, rolling it" into the box. A key moment occurs when a USA player, identified by a commentator as "Horan" and later by another as "Lindsey" who "jumps into it and initiates the contact," is tackled near the penalty area, leading to a loud whistle and a foul call by the Referee Team in bright green uniforms. USA players react with frustration, while a commentator discusses the "push from behind" and another concludes there's "no penalty." The play continues with "Done" executing a "spin move" and taking a "shot," followed by "Rapino" sending the ball up, which is "headed by Lavelle" and then "Saved by the goalkeeper!" on a "rebound done." The scene transitions to the half-time break, with the scoreboard confirming the 3-0 lead and listing the goal scorers: Morgan (12'), Lavelle (20'), and Horan (32'). The camera then cuts to a crowd of enthusiastic fans at the Lehigh Valley Soccerfest in Bethlehem, PA, celebrating the USA's performance, waving flags, and cheering loudly, including one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement, creating an electric atmosphere. The segment ends with a wide shot of the stadium, showing the players walking off the field as the half-time graphics display the score and goal details, with the crowd still audible.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures the final moments of the first half of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match between the USA and Thailand, with the scoreline at 3-0 in favor of the USA. The camera follows the play closely, showing a Thai defender in light blue intercepting the ball and clearing it. The USA continues to press, with multiple players in red jerseys making runs toward the goal, while the Thai goalkeeper in yellow remains alert. The camera then cuts to a crowd of enthusiastic fans at the Lehigh Valley Soccerfest in Bethlehem, PA, celebrating the USA's performance, waving flags, and cheering. The atmosphere is electric, with fans in USA jerseys and patriotic attire, including one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement. The segment ends with a wide shot of the stadium, showing the players walking off the field as the half-time graphics display the score and goal details.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide aerial view of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 Group F match, showing the USA in red jerseys dominating Thailand in light blue, with the scoreboard indicating a 3-0 lead. The camera then cuts to the USA bench, where a coach and another woman clap, reflecting the team's strong momentum. A male commentator's voice guides the action, describing a play that leads to a pivotal moment: "Finds Mewis, Mewis with the shot deflected, GOAL!" The stadium erupts in a loud roar as the commentator exclaims, "Four nothing! The US!" Visually, Megan Rapinoe, identifiable by her pink hair and number 15 jersey, is seen celebrating joyfully with teammate Alex Morgan (#13), while the scoreboard updates to 4-0. A replay of the goal shows Rapinoe's involvement in the powerful shot that beats the Thai goalkeeper in yellow, with the commentator clarifying that Sam Mewis, who he praises for her recent rise to stardom, scored the goal, assisted by Rapinoe. The electric atmosphere continues with fans waving American flags and cheering loudly. Soon after, another goal is scored, bringing the score to 5-0. The commentator describes the build-up, mentioning "Heath" and "Horan" before a "quick shot, GOAL!" and then confirms, "Alex Morgan's got two," aligning with the visual of Morgan scoring with a well-placed shot and celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives. The USA maintains control of the field, with the scoreboard visible at 53:06. The segment concludes with USA Player #3, a blonde player, walking off the field during the half-time break, as the vibrant stadium, adorned with Qatar Airways and Wanda banners, buzzes with the continuous cheers of fans and the FOX broadcast logo indicating live coverage.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a dominant performance by the USA women's soccer team during the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019, specifically in Group F against Thailand. The action begins with a wide aerial view of the pitch, showing USA in red jerseys and Thailand in light blue, with the scoreboard indicating a 3-0 lead for the USA at 49:26. The scene transitions to a close-up of the USA bench, where a coach in a gray USA-branded jacket and a woman in a blue jacket are clapping, reflecting the team's momentum. The focus then shifts to Megan Rapinoe, identifiable by her pink hair and red jersey with 'RAPINOE' and number 15, celebrating a goal with a joyful embrace from teammate Alex Morgan, who wears number 13. The scoreboard updates to 4-0 at 59:33, followed by a replay of the goal where Rapinoe scores with a powerful shot past the Thai goalkeeper in yellow. The crowd erupts in celebration, with fans waving American flags and cheering. The video then shows a subsequent goal, bringing the score to 5-0 at 52:50, with Alex Morgan scoring after a well-placed shot that the Thai goalkeeper fails to save. Morgan celebrates with a leap and high-fives, while the crowd continues to cheer. The final moments show a wide-angle view of the field as the USA maintains control, with the scoreboard at 53:06, and a close-up of USA Player #3, a blonde player in red jersey number 3, walking off the field during the half-time break. The stadium is filled with vibrant energy, with banners for Qatar Airways and Wanda visible, and the FOX broadcast logo indicating live coverage. The atmosphere is electric, with fans in the stands waving flags and celebrating the USA's commanding lead.

segments.integrated_caption: The FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match against Thailand is in full swing, with the USA women's soccer team showcasing a dominant performance, as the score progresses from 5-0 to 7-0 amidst the loud cheering and roaring stadium ambiance. The action begins with USA player Alex Morgan (#13) powerfully striking the ball into the net, with the Thai goalkeeper in yellow diving but failing to stop it. The crowd erupts in celebration, with fans waving American flags and cheering loudly as Alex Morgan embraces a teammate and runs with arms raised in triumph. The commentator notes the "US lead" as another goal is scored by USA player #11, identified by the commentator as Lavelle, who scores after a well-placed shot, again past the Thai goalkeeper. The scoreboard quickly updates to 6-0, then 7-0, with the commentator exclaiming "Six nothing" and later "Seven nothing US" and confirming "Lavelle has two" goals, as the USA team maintains relentless pressure. Thai players in light blue are seen defending and reacting to the attacks, with one player attempting to intercept the ball and another involved in a tackle that leads to a foul, signaled by the female referee in green. The stadium is filled with energy, with banners for sponsors like Qatar Airways, Adidas, and EDF visible around the pitch, and the continuous roar of the crowd underscores the excitement as the Thai goalkeeper stands in goal and the match progresses into the 56th minute, with USA players displaying joy and confidence.

The final moments show the USA team continuing to press, with the Thai goalkeeper standing in goal as the match progresses into the 56th minute. The atmosphere is electric, with fans in the stands screaming and celebrating each goal, and the USA players displaying joy and confidence on the field.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the Stadium Crowd, where fans are visibly engaged, some with American flag face paint and others holding drinks, reflecting the intense atmosphere of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match. The scoreboard shows USA leading 7-0 against Thailand at 60:00. Commentary notes a "Lloyd's turn" and a "great goal" from earlier, adding that "she has 30 minutes to equal that." The camera then cuts to the field, where USA Player #11 is seen in action, followed by a wide shot of the pitch as the USA team advances. The Thai National Team, in light blue jerseys, attempts to defend, with commentary describing a shot "shooting low" that's "blocked" and "cleared out," with the "last touch by Chinwong." A subsequent shot is "well wide of the target." The commentary then shifts to a more conversational tone, remarking, "Well, you could laugh when you have nothing, right?" in reference to the lopsided score. Visually, USA Player #13, Alex Morgan, scores a goal with a powerful strike past the Thai Goalkeeper, celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives. A close-up shows a dejected Thai player, M. Chinwong, while USA Player #8 with blue hair runs in celebration. The USA Coaching Staff appears concerned. Commentary then describes Carli Lloyd attempting to "turn and spin" and shooting, followed by a "save" that mentions "Morgan!" before going "wide." The match continues with Alex Morgan visually scoring again, the Thai Goalkeeper diving but failing to save, and the referee signaling the goal. Commentary highlights "so much offense out there tonight for the US," mentioning "Rapinoe" clearing the ball and later "Pugh taking out Ertz," leading to "an easy foul to call." The final moments show a tackle near the penalty area, resulting in a foul called by the Referee Team, with the VAR Assistant Referee holding a flag. The scoreboard updates to 7-0, and the crowd's energy remains high, with fans waving flags and cheering, as the commentary concludes with a mention of "Thailand."

The camera then cuts to the field, where USA Player #11, wearing a red jersey with the number 11, is seen in action, followed by a wide shot of the pitch as the USA team advances. The Thai National Team, in light blue jerseys, attempts to defend, but USA Player #13, Alex Morgan, scores a goal with a powerful strike, sending the ball past the Thai Goalkeeper in yellow. The goal is celebrated with a joyful leap and high-fives. The scene shifts to a close-up of a Thai player, M. Chinwong, looking dejected, while USA Player #8, with distinctive blue hair, runs in celebration. The camera then focuses on the USA Coaching Staff, a man in a dark suit, who appears concerned. The match continues with USA Player #13, Alex Morgan, scoring again, and the Thai Goalkeeper diving but failing to save. The referee, in a bright green uniform, signals the goal. The final moments show a tackle near the penalty area, resulting in a foul called by the Referee Team, with the VAR Assistant Referee holding a flag. The scoreboard updates to 7-0, and the crowd's energy remains high, with fans waving flags and cheering.

segments.integrated_caption: The FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between USA and Thailand showcases a dominant USA performance, with the score progressing from 7-0 to 9-0 amidst a vibrant stadium atmosphere filled with cheering spectators. The segment opens with a commentator discussing a potential booking and a quick play involving a player identified as 'Horan.' A powerful shot from USA's Alex Morgan (#13, in red) results in a goal, with the commentator excitedly exclaiming 'quick shot! And there's another! Alex Morgan!' and declaring 'It's her night.' The scoreboard updates to 8-0, and Morgan celebrates joyfully, raising her arms, while a dejected Thai defender, M. Chinwong (#3), walks away. The commentator highlights this as 'Most goals US has scored in a single World Cup game.' The action continues with the commentator mentioning a Thai player, possibly 'Intamee,' and praising 'O'Hara' for good defensive work. Maintaining relentless offensive pressure, USA's Megan Rapinoe (#15, with distinctive pink hair) scores another goal after a quick counter-attack, the ball rolling past the diving Thai goalkeeper. The commentator's excitement peaks, announcing 'Rapinoe, who runs on! Rapinoe with the goal! Nine nothing US!' Rapinoe celebrates with a leap and high-five as the Thai goalkeeper remains on the ground. On-screen graphics display the overwhelming score and tournament branding, including sponsor logos, as the USA players continue their advance, leaving a frustrated Thai defender (#7) in their wake, all underscored by the continuous crowd noise and enthusiastic commentary.

Chinwong, #3) looking dejected as she walks away from the goal. The sequence continues with another USA goal, this time from a player with pink hair (Megan Rapinoe, #15), who scores after a quick counter-attack, with the ball rolling past the diving Thai goalkeeper. The scoreboard updates to 9-0, and Rapinoe is shown celebrating with a leap and a high-five. The Thai goalkeeper, in a yellow jersey, is seen on the ground after the save attempt, while the USA players continue their offensive pressure. The final moments show a wide view of the field with USA players advancing, and a close-up of a Thai defender (#7) looking frustrated. The stadium is filled with spectators, and the on-screen graphics display the match time, score, and tournament branding, including 'FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019' and sponsor logos like VISA and WANDA. The atmosphere is intense, with the USA team maintaining control and the Thai team struggling to defend.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the loud, celebratory roar of the stadium crowd, their cheers accompanying the USA women's soccer team in red jerseys as they huddle in joy after a goal, while the Thai team in light blue appears dejected. The commentator's voice cuts through the excitement, noting the relentless pressure and exclaiming, "quick shot taken!" followed by "She's got four! Alex Morgan!" as USA's Alex Morgan (number 13) scores, celebrating with arms raised. The scoreboard updates to 10-0, intensifying the crowd's enthusiasm, with fans waving American flags. The commentator then describes a missed opportunity by "Carli Lloyd," remarking on its "uncharacteristic" nature. The action quickly shifts back to the USA's attack, with Morgan nodding the ball for "Pugh," USA Player #11, who takes a well-placed shot into an "open net." The commentator excitedly announces, "There's the 11! Her first World Cup goal!" The Thai players, including M. Chinwong (number 3), look disheartened amidst the USA's continued celebration. The electric atmosphere of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 is palpable, with banners for sponsors and FOX broadcast graphics displaying the live time and the historic 11-0 scoreline.

The camera then follows the action as USA players maintain relentless pressure, with Alex Morgan (number 13) scoring a goal after a swift break, followed by a close-up of her celebrating with arms raised. The next goal comes from a powerful shot by another USA player, with the Thai goalkeeper in yellow diving but failing to save it. The scoreboard updates to 10-0, and the crowd erupts in celebration, with fans waving American flags and cheering. Chinwong (number 3), look disheartened, while the USA players continue to celebrate. The stadium is filled with energy, with banners for VISA, Qatar Airways, and Adidas visible around the pitch, and the FOX broadcast graphics display the live time and score. The atmosphere is electric, with the USA team showcasing their dominance and the crowd fully engaged in the historic performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment captures the USA women's soccer team's dominant performance in the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match against Thailand, with the score escalating from 11-0 to 13-0 amidst a backdrop of loud crowd cheers and an excited commentator exclaiming, "up, debut!" The action begins with a USA player in red, Megan Rapinoe (#15), scoring a goal after a swift attack, followed by a close-up of her celebrating with teammates, including Alex Morgan (#13), who embraces her. The camera then cuts to the USA bench, where the coaching staff and substitutes, including a coach in a blue jacket, erupt in celebration. The scene shifts to a wide-angle view of the field, showing the USA team maintaining relentless pressure. The commentator's voice rises, announcing, "Morgan, shot! Goal!" as Alex Morgan scores, making it 12-0. The crowd's cheers are deafening as the commentator declares, "Five for Morgan!" and notes she's "tying the great Michelle Akers with a five-goal performance." A female commentator adds, "And this is just ridiculous," analyzing the play. The dominance continues, with the commentator building anticipation, "Lloyd has a chance here!" before exclaiming, "Carli Lloyd, she's got it!" as another USA player, identified as Carli Lloyd, scores past the diving Thai goalkeeper in yellow. The crowd erupts again, and the commentator announces the staggering score, "13 nothing US!" Rapinoe and Morgan are seen hugging and running joyfully amidst the sustained cheers. The on-screen graphics consistently display the match time, score, and tournament branding. The segment concludes with a dejected Thai player, M. Chinwong (#3), as the USA's confidence and skill remain on full display, cementing their overwhelming victory.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a dominant performance by the USA women's soccer team in the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match against Thailand, with the scoreline escalating from 11-0 to 13-0. The action begins with a USA player in red (Megan Rapinoe, #15) scoring a goal after a swift attack, followed by a close-up of her celebrating with teammates, including Alex Morgan (#13), who is seen embracing her. The camera then cuts to the USA bench, where the coaching staff and substitutes erupt in celebration, with one coach in a blue jacket jumping in excitement. The scene shifts to a wide-angle view of the field, showing the USA team maintaining relentless pressure, with multiple players in red advancing toward the Thai goal. A subsequent goal is scored by another USA player, with the ball finding the net past the Thai goalkeeper in yellow, who dives but fails to stop it. The USA players celebrate again, with Rapinoe and Morgan hugging and running joyfully. The crowd in the stadium is visibly energized, waving flags and cheering. The final moments show a Thai player in light blue (M. Chinwong, #3) looking dejected as the USA continues to dominate, with the scoreboard reflecting the 13-0 lead. The on-screen graphics consistently display the match time, score, and tournament branding, including 'FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019' and sponsor logos like Qatar Airways and VISA. The atmosphere is electric, with the USA team’s confidence and skill on full display.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the roar of a cheering crowd as USA Player #10, identified by a commentator as LLOYD, dribbles the ball and scores the final goal, the ball flying past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. Enthusiastic USA fans, including one in a patriotic hat, are seen cheering loudly in the stands. As the match enters stoppage time, the scoreboard displays 92:01, with commentators discussing Lloyd's tactical shift into a 'double nine spot' and another player, Pew, shifting into midfield. A distinct whistle signals the 'Final whistle,' prompting a commentator to exclaim, 'What a night!' and highlight the unprecedented '13 nothing results at a World Cup.' Players from both the USA and Thai National Teams, in their red and light blue kits respectively, begin to shake hands, displaying sportsmanship. The final score, 'FULL TIME USA 13-0 THAILAND,' is displayed alongside a scrolling list of goal scorers, including Alex Morgan, who a commentator explicitly credits with 'five goals.' USA players, including Megan Rapinoe with pink hair and USA Player #8 with blue hair, embrace teammates and staff, with a coach in a blue jacket jumping in celebration. The Thai Goalkeeper is seen walking off with a USA player, a gesture of mutual respect, before the screen transitions to a red 'Thanks For Watching Subscribe Now!' message.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures the final moments of a dominant USA victory in the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match against Thailand, with the scoreline at 13-0. The sequence begins with a close-up of a USA player in a red jersey (number 10, identified as LLOYD) dribbling the ball before a shot is taken, resulting in a goal as the ball flies past the Thai goalkeeper in yellow, who dives but fails to save it. The camera then cuts to the crowd, where fans in USA-themed attire, including one woman in a red jersey and another wearing a patriotic hat, cheer enthusiastically. The scene shifts to a wide view of the field during stoppage time, showing players from both teams in their respective red and light blue kits, with the scoreboard indicating 92:01 and +3 minutes added. As the match concludes, players from both teams begin to shake hands and show sportsmanship, with USA players in red jerseys and Thai players in light blue jerseys exchanging gestures of respect. The final score is displayed as 'FULL TIME USA 13-0 THAILAND', with a scrolling list of goal scorers including Morgan, Lavelle, Horan, Mewis, and others. The USA players, including one with pink hair (Megan Rapinoe) and another with blue hair (USA Player #8), are seen embracing teammates and staff, including a coach in a blue jacket who jumps in celebration. The Thai goalkeeper, in a yellow jersey, is shown walking off the field with a USA player, displaying mutual respect. The segment ends with a red screen displaying 'Thanks For Watching Subscribe Now!' in white text.
